title: Right at Home Tri-County Metro Case Study: Driving Growth and Clinical Value with Data-Driven Care
description: How Jeff Welsh, owner of Right at Home Tri-County Metro, uses Able Assess to close the assessment gap, strengthen referral relationships with primary care physicians and physical therapists, and turn community screenings into a differentiated growth engine.

seo.title: Right at Home Tri-County Metro Case Study | Able Care
seo.description: How Jeff Welsh uses Able Assess to bridge the assessment gap, strengthen the healthcare ecosystem and redefine market differentiation for Right at Home Tri-County Metro.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Older adult completing an Able Assess screening at home
blocks.eyebrow: Case Study: Right at Home Tri-County Metro
blocks.headline: Driving growth and clinical value with data-driven care.
blocks.subtitle: Jeff Welsh, owner of Right at Home Tri-County Metro, on how Able Assess bridges the assessment gap, strengthens the healthcare ecosystem and redefines what differentiation looks like in home care.

blocks.heading: An innovative leader bridging compassion and clinical precision
blocks.body: Jeff Welsh, the owner of Right at Home Tri-County Metro, is an innovative leader who bridges the gap between compassionate care and clinical precision. By integrating technology like Able Assess, he has built his agency into a forward-thinking organization that delivers measurable results, moving beyond traditional, static care models to focus on longevity and independence.
blocks.portraitAlt: Jeff Welsh, Owner of Right at Home Tri-County Metro
blocks.name: Jeff Welsh
blocks.role: Owner, Right at Home Tri-County Metro
blocks.highlightQuote: Our numbers show that if we get to that assessment, the chances that you will convert to an actual client are actually very good.

blocks.heading: Bridging the assessment gap
blocks.body:
One of the most pressing challenges Jeff identified within his operations was the “assessment gap”—the friction point between a family’s initial inquiry and the actual in-home evaluation. Jeff noted that while his team is exceptional at fielding calls, only about a third of those inquiries typically transition to the next step.

To bridge this, Jeff integrated Able Assess as a cornerstone of his sales strategy, viewing the technology as a way to offer immediate, hospital-grade value before a contract is even signed. By promising a free, sophisticated fall-risk screening, Jeff believes he can push his assessment conversion rate from 40% to a target of 60%.

For Jeff, this isn’t just about the math; it’s about providing families with objective insight that offers instant clarity during a high-stress decision-making process.

testimonials.quote: So now if a customer is calling and talking to this home care company, maybe they’re going to talk to three of them, and going to decide who’s going to come to the house. And I give them an advantage like Able Assess. I think it’s going to increase the likelihood that I will get a chance to make that assessment and change that metric. Our numbers show that if we get to that assessment, the chances that you will convert to an actual client are actually very good.
testimonials.name: Jeff Welsh
testimonials.role: Owner, Right at Home Tri-County Metro

blocks.heading: Strengthening the healthcare ecosystem
blocks.body:
Beyond the initial sale, Jeff’s vision extends to the entire healthcare ecosystem. He recognizes that home care should not exist in a vacuum, and he uses the data generated by Able Assess to transform his agency into a vital clinical partner for primary care physicians and skilled therapy providers.

When Jeff’s team identifies a decline in a client’s grip strength or gait speed through the Able Assess portal, they proactively reach out to primary care physicians and physical therapists. This data-driven advocacy allows healthcare partners to “swoop in” with the necessary interventions, creating a reciprocal relationship built on professional trust.

This approach has turned his agency into a facilitator of wider care, ensuring that his clients are safer while his professional partners view Right at Home as a sophisticated, essential ally.

testimonials.quote: Making referrals to skilled therapy providers improves the value that you present to your professional healthcare partners that you work with by giving them business when you see signs of weakness and potential for falls.
testimonials.name: Jeff Welsh
testimonials.role: Owner, Right at Home Tri-County Metro

blocks.heading: Redefining market differentiation
blocks.body:
This commitment to differentiation is most visible in how Jeff handles community outreach. Rather than standard marketing, Jeff utilizes group screenings at independent and assisted living communities as a powerful reason for engagement. These events allow him to offer a genuine service to residents while simultaneously building a pipeline of prospective clients who now see Right at Home as an expert in fall prevention.

By focusing on the “why”—the simplicity, speed and immense value of the information—Jeff has moved his business beyond the traditional model of “selling hours.” Instead, he is selling a holistic care plan backed by trend data and objective proof, ensuring that every client under his care isn’t just being watched, but is actually getting stronger.

blocks.heading: A future-focused mission
blocks.body: Ultimately, Jeff Welsh’s adoption of Able Assess is a natural extension of his mission to be a “navigator” for families facing the complexities of aging. As he continues to scale these innovations across the Tri-County Metro area, Jeff remains focused on the one outcome that truly matters: helping seniors stay safe, independent and exactly where they want to be—at home.

blocks.heading: See how Able Assess changes the inquiry conversation
blocks.body: 30 minutes with the Able Care clinical team. We’ll walk through the workflow, the monthly cadence and the modeling behind the conversion lift.
